{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11393857]] Wikidata name: naishi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Naishi in Japan|Naesi in Korea|{{ill|Eunuch#Korea|ja|宦官#朝鮮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eunuch#Korea|qq=|1=eunuch official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24841818}}}} '''Naishi''' (内侍) refers to a category of {{ill|female positions|ja|女性職|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/female positions|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in premodern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The term "Naishi" encompasses the following:
# The female officials who served in the {{ill|Naishi-no-tsukasa|ja|内侍司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-no-tsukasa|qq=|1=Naishi-no-tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}}, an office of the {{ill|Imperial Court|ja|後宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|qq=|1=back palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5353683}} under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. # A type of female official in the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}}. # A type of female official in the {{ill|Saiin|ja|斎院司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saiin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. # A type of [[:en:Miko|Miko]] (shrine maiden) at the [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]].
== Naishi of the Naishi-no-tsukasa == The Naishi who served in the {{ill|Naishi-no-tsukasa|ja|内侍司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-no-tsukasa|qq=|1=Naishi-no-tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}} were tasked with closely attending to the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor]], handling duties such as mediating {{ill|sōjō|ja|奏上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōjō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上奏|1=sōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358776}} (reports to the emperor), conveying the emperor's {{ill|Senka|ja|宣下|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senka|qq=|1=Senka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452917}} (imperial decrees), and managing the {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro|ja|賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|2=ja|3=賢所|4=fr|5=Kashiko-dokoro|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|qq=}}.
The head of the Naishi-no-tsukasa was called the {{ill|Shōji|ja|尚侍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōji|qq=|1=Naishi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11039254}} (alternatively read as "Naishi-no-kami"), with a limit of two appointments. The second-Ranking officials were the {{ill|Tenji|ja|典侍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji|qq=|1=Naishi-no-suke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10894614}} (Naishi-no-suke), with four appointments, while the third-Ranking officials, known as {{ill|Shōji|ja|掌侍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōji|qq=|1=Naishi-no-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498168}} (Naishi-no-jō), could also be referred to as "Naishi" in a narrower sense. There were four Shōji positions. Although there were no {{ill|Shuten|ja|主典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四等官|1=shitōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}} (clerks), the office followed a {{ill|Four-level official hierarchy|ja|四等官|1=shitōkan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-level official hierarchy|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan制|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}}.
In addition, there were {{ill|Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja|権掌侍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ("provisional Naishi-no-jō") positions with two appointments, as well as an ad hoc position called {{ill|Naishi-dai|ja|内侍代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-dai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to temporarily replace absent Naishi. Although no Naishi roles were directly assigned to the {{ill|Crown Prince's Household|ja|春宮坊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crown Prince's Household|qq=|1=Crown Prince's Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513463}}, the Naishi-dai was permanently stationed there and would typically transition to a full Naishi position upon the prince’s enthronement.
== Naishi of the Saikū == These Naishi served in close proximity to the [[:en:Saikū|Saikū]], attending to her needs and responsibilities.
== Naishi of the Saiin == Similarly, the Naishi in the [[:en:Saiin (priestess)|Saiin]] served the [[:en:Saiō|Saio]] in a comparable capacity to those of the Saikū.
== Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine == At [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]], the Naishi were female shrine priests ([[:en:Miko|Miko]]). Their duties included participating in rituals, reciting {{ill|Imayō|ja|今様|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imayō|qq=|1=imayō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985039}} to entertain noble guests who lodged at the shrine, and performing {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bugaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舞|1=mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109903880}}. According to the "Itsukushima Michishiba-ki" ({{ill|厳島道芝記|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/厳島道芝記|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine maintained a hierarchical structure of Naishi from first Rank (一﨟) to eighth Rank (八﨟), collectively referred to as the "Yaotome" (八乙女) or "Hon-Naishi" (本内侍). Additional titles such as "Wagoto-Naishi" (和琴内侍) and "Karagami-Naishi" (韓神内侍) indicate a system of more than ten positions.
It is also said that {{ill|Mikohimegimi|ja|御子姫君|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikohimegimi|qq=|1=御子姫君|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488907}}, the seventh daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, was born to Kiyomori and a Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine during his tenure as the governor of Aki Province.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内侍]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11393857]] Wikidata name: naishi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Naishi in Japan|Naesi in Korea|{{ill|Eunuch#Korea|ja|宦官#朝鮮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eunuch#Korea|qq=|1=eunuch official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24841818}}}} '''Naishi''' (内侍) refers to a category of {{ill|female positions|ja|女性職|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/female positions|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in premodern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The term "Naishi" encompasses the following:
# The female officials who served in the {{ill|Naishi-no-tsukasa|ja|内侍司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-no-tsukasa|qq=|1=Naishi-no-tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}}, an office of the {{ill|Imperial Court|ja|後宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|qq=|1=back palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5353683}} under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. # A type of female official in the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}}. # A type of female official in the {{ill|Saiin|ja|斎院司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saiin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. # A type of [[:en:Miko|Miko]] (shrine maiden) at the [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]].
== Naishi of the Naishi-no-tsukasa == The Naishi who served in the {{ill|Naishi-no-tsukasa|ja|内侍司|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-no-tsukasa|qq=|1=Naishi-no-tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}} were tasked with closely attending to the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor]], handling duties such as mediating {{ill|sōjō|ja|奏上|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōjō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上奏|1=sōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358776}} (reports to the emperor), conveying the emperor's {{ill|Senka|ja|宣下|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senka|qq=|1=Senka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452917}} (imperial decrees), and managing the {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro|ja|賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|2=ja|3=賢所|4=fr|5=Kashiko-dokoro|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|qq=}}.
The head of the Naishi-no-tsukasa was called the {{ill|Shōji|ja|尚侍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōji|qq=|1=Naishi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11039254}} (alternatively read as "Naishi-no-kami"), with a limit of two appointments. The second-Ranking officials were the {{ill|Tenji|ja|典侍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji|qq=|1=Naishi-no-suke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10894614}} (Naishi-no-suke), with four appointments, while the third-Ranking officials, known as {{ill|Shōji|ja|掌侍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōji|qq=|1=Naishi-no-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498168}} (Naishi-no-jō), could also be referred to as "Naishi" in a narrower sense. There were four Shōji positions. Although there were no {{ill|Shuten|ja|主典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四等官|1=shitōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}} (clerks), the office followed a {{ill|Four-level official hierarchy|ja|四等官|1=shitōkan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-level official hierarchy|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan制|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}}.
In addition, there were {{ill|Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja|権掌侍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ("provisional Naishi-no-jō") positions with two appointments, as well as an ad hoc position called {{ill|Naishi-dai|ja|内侍代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-dai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to temporarily replace absent Naishi. Although no Naishi roles were directly assigned to the {{ill|Crown Prince's Household|ja|春宮坊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crown Prince's Household|qq=|1=Crown Prince's Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513463}}, the Naishi-dai was permanently stationed there and would typically transition to a full Naishi position upon the prince’s enthronement.
== Naishi of the Saikū == These Naishi served in close proximity to the [[:en:Saikū|Saikū]], attending to her needs and responsibilities.
== Naishi of the Saiin == Similarly, the Naishi in the [[:en:Saiin (priestess)|Saiin]] served the [[:en:Saiō|Saio]] in a comparable capacity to those of the Saikū.
== Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine == At [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]], the Naishi were female shrine priests ([[:en:Miko|Miko]]). Their duties included participating in rituals, reciting {{ill|Imayō|ja|今様|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imayō|qq=|1=imayō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985039}} to entertain noble guests who lodged at the shrine, and performing {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bugaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舞|1=mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109903880}}. According to the "Itsukushima Michishiba-ki" ({{ill|厳島道芝記|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/厳島道芝記|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine maintained a hierarchical structure of Naishi from first Rank (一﨟) to eighth Rank (八﨟), collectively referred to as the "Yaotome" (八乙女) or "Hon-Naishi" (本内侍). Additional titles such as "Wagoto-Naishi" (和琴内侍) and "Karagami-Naishi" (韓神内侍) indicate a system of more than ten positions.
It is also said that {{ill|Mikohimegimi|ja|御子姫君|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikohimegimi|qq=|1=御子姫君|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488907}}, the seventh daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, was born to Kiyomori and a Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine during his tenure as the governor of Aki Province.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|author=[[Miyachi Naoichi]] and [[Saeki Ariyoshi]] (supervisors)|title=Shinto Daijiten (Abridged Edition)|publisher=[[Rinsen Shoten]]|date=1969|isbn=4-653-01347-0|original=Heibonsha, 1940}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

The term "Naishi" encompasses the following:
# The female officials who served in the {{ill|Naishi-no-tsukasa|ja|内侍司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-no-tsukasa|1=Naishi-no-tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}}, an office of the {{ill|Imperial Court|ja|後宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|1=back palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5353683}} under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. # A type of female official in the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}}. # A type of female official in the {{ill|Saiin|ja|斎院司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saiin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. # A type of [[:en:Miko|Miko]] (shrine maiden) at the [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]].
== Naishi of the Naishi-no-tsukasa == The Naishi who served in the {{ill|Naishi-no-tsukasa|ja|内侍司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-no-tsukasa|1=Naishi-no-tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}} were tasked with closely attending to the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor]], handling duties such as mediating {{ill|sōjō|ja|奏上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上奏|1=sōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358776}} (reports to the emperor), conveying the emperor's {{ill|Senka|ja|宣下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senka|1=Senka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452917}} (imperial decrees), and managing the {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro|ja|賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|2=ja|3=賢所|4=fr|5=Kashiko-dokoro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|qq=}}.
The head of the Naishi-no-tsukasa was called the {{ill|Shōji|ja|尚侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōji|1=Naishi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11039254}} (alternatively read as "Naishi-no-kami"), with a limit of two appointments. The second-Ranking officials were the {{ill|Tenji|ja|典侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji|1=Naishi-no-suke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10894614}} (Naishi-no-suke), with four appointments, while the third-Ranking officials, known as {{ill|Shōji|ja|掌侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōji|1=Naishi-no-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498168}} (Naishi-no-jō), could also be referred to as "Naishi" in a narrower sense. There were four Shōji positions. Although there were no {{ill|Shuten|ja|主典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四等官|1=shitōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}} (clerks), the office followed a {{ill|Four-level official hierarchy|ja|四等官|1=shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-level official hierarchy|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan制|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}}.
In addition, there were {{ill|Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja|権掌侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ("provisional Naishi-no-jō") positions with two appointments, as well as an ad hoc position called {{ill|Naishi-dai|ja|内侍代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-dai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to temporarily replace absent Naishi. Although no Naishi roles were directly assigned to the {{ill|Crown Prince's Household|ja|春宮坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crown Prince's Household|1=Crown Prince's Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513463}}, the Naishi-dai was permanently stationed there and would typically transition to a full Naishi position upon the prince’s enthronement.
== Naishi of the Saikū == These Naishi served in close proximity to the [[:en:Saikū|Saikū]], attending to her needs and responsibilities.
== Naishi of the Saiin == Similarly, the Naishi in the [[:en:Saiin (priestess)|Saiin]] served the [[:en:Saiō|Saio]] in a comparable capacity to those of the Saikū.
== Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine == At [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]], the Naishi were female shrine priests ([[:en:Miko|Miko]]). Their duties included participating in rituals, reciting {{ill|Imayō|ja|今様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imayō|1=imayō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985039}} to entertain noble guests who lodged at the shrine, and performing {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bugaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舞|1=mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109903880}}. According to the "Itsukushima Michishiba-ki" ({{ill|厳島道芝記|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/厳島道芝記|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine maintained a hierarchical structure of Naishi from first Rank (一﨟) to eighth Rank (八﨟), collectively referred to as the "Yaotome" (八乙女) or "Hon-Naishi" (本内侍). Additional titles such as "Wagoto-Naishi" (和琴内侍) and "Karagami-Naishi" (韓神内侍) indicate a system of more than ten positions.
It is also said that {{ill|Mikohimegimi|ja|御子姫君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikohimegimi|1=御子姫君|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488907}}, the seventh daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, was born to Kiyomori and a Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine during his tenure as the governor of Aki Province.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|author=[[Miyachi Naoichi]] and [[Saeki Ariyoshi]] (supervisors)|title=Shinto Daijiten (Abridged Edition)|publisher=[[Rinsen Shoten]]|date=1969|isbn=4-653-01347-0|original=Heibonsha, 1940}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

== Naishi of the Naishi-no-tsukasa == The Naishi who served in the {{ill|Naishi-no-tsukasa|ja|内侍司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-no-tsukasa|1=Naishi-no-tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}} were tasked with closely attending to the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor]], handling duties such as mediating {{ill|sōjō|ja|奏上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上奏|1=sōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358776}} (reports to the emperor), conveying the emperor's {{ill|Senka|ja|宣下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senka|1=Senka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452917}} (imperial decrees), and managing the {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro|ja|賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|2=ja|3=賢所|4=fr|5=Kashiko-dokoro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|qq=}}.
The head of the Naishi-no-tsukasa was called the {{ill|Shōji|ja|尚侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōji|1=Naishi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11039254}} (alternatively read as "Naishi-no-kami"), with a limit of two appointments. The second-Ranking officials were the {{ill|Tenji|ja|典侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji|1=Naishi-no-suke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10894614}} (Naishi-no-suke), with four appointments, while the third-Ranking officials, known as {{ill|Shōji|ja|掌侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōji|1=Naishi-no-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498168}} (Naishi-no-jō), could also be referred to as "Naishi" in a narrower sense. There were four Shōji positions. Although there were no {{ill|Shuten|ja|主典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四等官|1=shitōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}} (clerks), the office followed a {{ill|Four-level official hierarchy|ja|四等官|1=shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-level official hierarchy|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan制|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}}.
In addition, there were {{ill|Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja|権掌侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ("provisional Naishi-no-jō") positions with two appointments, as well as an ad hoc position called {{ill|Naishi-dai|ja|内侍代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-dai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to temporarily replace absent Naishi. Although no Naishi roles were directly assigned to the {{ill|Crown Prince's Household|ja|春宮坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crown Prince's Household|1=Crown Prince's Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513463}}, the Naishi-dai was permanently stationed there and would typically transition to a full Naishi position upon the prince’s enthronement.
== Naishi of the Saikū == These Naishi served in close proximity to the [[:en:Saikū|Saikū]], attending to her needs and responsibilities.
== Naishi of the Saiin == Similarly, the Naishi in the [[:en:Saiin (priestess)|Saiin]] served the [[:en:Saiō|Saio]] in a comparable capacity to those of the Saikū.
== Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine == At [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]], the Naishi were female shrine priests ([[:en:Miko|Miko]]). Their duties included participating in rituals, reciting {{ill|Imayō|ja|今様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imayō|1=imayō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985039}} to entertain noble guests who lodged at the shrine, and performing {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bugaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舞|1=mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109903880}}. According to the "Itsukushima Michishiba-ki" ({{ill|厳島道芝記|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/厳島道芝記|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine maintained a hierarchical structure of Naishi from first Rank (一﨟) to eighth Rank (八﨟), collectively referred to as the "Yaotome" (八乙女) or "Hon-Naishi" (本内侍). Additional titles such as "Wagoto-Naishi" (和琴内侍) and "Karagami-Naishi" (韓神内侍) indicate a system of more than ten positions.
It is also said that {{ill|Mikohimegimi|ja|御子姫君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikohimegimi|1=御子姫君|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488907}}, the seventh daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, was born to Kiyomori and a Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine during his tenure as the governor of Aki Province.

== References == * {{Cite book|和書|author=[[Miyachi Naoichi]] and [[Saeki Ariyoshi]] (supervisors)|title=Shinto Daijiten (Abridged Edition)|publisher=[[Rinsen Shoten]]|date=1969|isbn=4-653-01347-0|original=Heibonsha, 1940}}

The second-Ranking officials were the {{ill|Tenji|ja|典侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji|1=Naishi-no-suke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10894614}} (Naishi-no-suke), with four appointments, while the third-Ranking officials, known as {{ill|Shōji|ja|掌侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōji|1=Naishi-no-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498168}} (Naishi-no-jō), could also be referred to as "Naishi" in a narrower sense. There were four Shōji positions. Although there were no {{ill|Shuten|ja|主典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四等官|1=shitōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}} (clerks), the office followed a {{ill|Four-level official hierarchy|ja|四等官|1=shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-level official hierarchy|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan制|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}}.
In addition, there were {{ill|Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja|権掌侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ("provisional Naishi-no-jō") positions with two appointments, as well as an ad hoc position called {{ill|Naishi-dai|ja|内侍代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-dai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to temporarily replace absent Naishi. Although no Naishi roles were directly assigned to the {{ill|Crown Prince's Household|ja|春宮坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crown Prince's Household|1=Crown Prince's Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513463}}, the Naishi-dai was permanently stationed there and would typically transition to a full Naishi position upon the prince’s enthronement.
== Naishi of the Saikū == These Naishi served in close proximity to the [[:en:Saikū|Saikū]], attending to her needs and responsibilities.
== Naishi of the Saiin == Similarly, the Naishi in the [[:en:Saiin (priestess)|Saiin]] served the [[:en:Saiō|Saio]] in a comparable capacity to those of the Saikū.
== Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine == At [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]], the Naishi were female shrine priests ([[:en:Miko|Miko]]). Their duties included participating in rituals, reciting {{ill|Imayō|ja|今様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imayō|1=imayō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985039}} to entertain noble guests who lodged at the shrine, and performing {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bugaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舞|1=mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109903880}}. According to the "Itsukushima Michishiba-ki" ({{ill|厳島道芝記|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/厳島道芝記|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine maintained a hierarchical structure of Naishi from first Rank (一﨟) to eighth Rank (八﨟), collectively referred to as the "Yaotome" (八乙女) or "Hon-Naishi" (本内侍). Additional titles such as "Wagoto-Naishi" (和琴内侍) and "Karagami-Naishi" (韓神内侍) indicate a system of more than ten positions.
It is also said that {{ill|Mikohimegimi|ja|御子姫君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikohimegimi|1=御子姫君|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488907}}, the seventh daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, was born to Kiyomori and a Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine during his tenure as the governor of Aki Province.

There were four Shōji positions. Although there were no {{ill|Shuten|ja|主典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四等官|1=shitōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}} (clerks), the office followed a {{ill|Four-level official hierarchy|ja|四等官|1=shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-level official hierarchy|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan制|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}}.
In addition, there were {{ill|Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja|権掌侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ("provisional Naishi-no-jō") positions with two appointments, as well as an ad hoc position called {{ill|Naishi-dai|ja|内侍代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-dai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to temporarily replace absent Naishi. Although no Naishi roles were directly assigned to the {{ill|Crown Prince's Household|ja|春宮坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crown Prince's Household|1=Crown Prince's Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513463}}, the Naishi-dai was permanently stationed there and would typically transition to a full Naishi position upon the prince’s enthronement.

== Naishi of the Saiin == Similarly, the Naishi in the [[:en:Saiin (priestess)|Saiin]] served the [[:en:Saiō|Saio]] in a comparable capacity to those of the Saikū.
== Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine == At [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]], the Naishi were female shrine priests ([[:en:Miko|Miko]]). Their duties included participating in rituals, reciting {{ill|Imayō|ja|今様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imayō|1=imayō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985039}} to entertain noble guests who lodged at the shrine, and performing {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bugaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舞|1=mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109903880}}. According to the "Itsukushima Michishiba-ki" ({{ill|厳島道芝記|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/厳島道芝記|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine maintained a hierarchical structure of Naishi from first Rank (一﨟) to eighth Rank (八﨟), collectively referred to as the "Yaotome" (八乙女) or "Hon-Naishi" (本内侍). Additional titles such as "Wagoto-Naishi" (和琴内侍) and "Karagami-Naishi" (韓神内侍) indicate a system of more than ten positions.
It is also said that {{ill|Mikohimegimi|ja|御子姫君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikohimegimi|1=御子姫君|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488907}}, the seventh daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, was born to Kiyomori and a Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine during his tenure as the governor of Aki Province.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内侍]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内侍]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内侍]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11393857]] Wikidata name: naishi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Naishi in Japan|Naesi in Korea|{{ill|Eunuch#Korea|ja|宦官#朝鮮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eunuch#Korea|1=eunuch official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24841818}}}} '''Naishi''' (内侍) refers to a category of {{ill|female positions|ja|女性職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/female positions|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in premodern [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The term "Naishi" encompasses the following:
# The female officials who served in the {{ill|Naishi-no-tsukasa|ja|内侍司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-no-tsukasa|1=Naishi-no-tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}}, an office of the {{ill|Imperial Court|ja|後宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|1=back palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5353683}} under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. # A type of female official in the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}}. # A type of female official in the {{ill|Saiin|ja|斎院司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saiin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. # A type of [[:en:Miko|Miko]] (shrine maiden) at the [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]] in [[:en:Aki Province|Aki Province]].
== Naishi of the Naishi-no-tsukasa == The Naishi who served in the {{ill|Naishi-no-tsukasa|ja|内侍司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-no-tsukasa|1=Naishi-no-tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}} were tasked with closely attending to the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|Emperor]], handling duties such as mediating {{ill|sōjō|ja|奏上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上奏|1=sōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358776}} (reports to the emperor), conveying the emperor's {{ill|Senka|ja|宣下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senka|1=Senka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452917}} (imperial decrees), and managing the {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro|ja|賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|2=ja|3=賢所|4=fr|5=Kashiko-dokoro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|qq=}}.
The head of the Naishi-no-tsukasa was called the {{ill|Shōji|ja|尚侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōji|1=Naishi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11039254}} (alternatively read as "Naishi-no-kami"), with a limit of two appointments. The second-Ranking officials were the {{ill|Tenji|ja|典侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji|1=Naishi-no-suke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10894614}} (Naishi-no-suke), with four appointments, while the third-Ranking officials, known as {{ill|Shōji|ja|掌侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōji|1=Naishi-no-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498168}} (Naishi-no-jō), could also be referred to as "Naishi" in a narrower sense. There were four Shōji positions. Although there were no {{ill|Shuten|ja|主典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四等官|1=shitōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}} (clerks), the office followed a {{ill|Four-level official hierarchy|ja|四等官|1=shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-level official hierarchy|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan制|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}}.
In addition, there were {{ill|Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja|権掌侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ("provisional Naishi-no-jō") positions with two appointments, as well as an ad hoc position called {{ill|Naishi-dai|ja|内侍代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-dai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to temporarily replace absent Naishi. Although no Naishi roles were directly assigned to the {{ill|Crown Prince's Household|ja|春宮坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crown Prince's Household|1=Crown Prince's Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513463}}, the Naishi-dai was permanently stationed there and would typically transition to a full Naishi position upon the prince’s enthronement.
== Naishi of the Saikū == These Naishi served in close proximity to the [[:en:Saikū|Saikū]], attending to her needs and responsibilities.
== Naishi of the Saiin == Similarly, the Naishi in the [[:en:Saiin (priestess)|Saiin]] served the [[:en:Saiō|Saio]] in a comparable capacity to those of the Saikū.
== Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine == At [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]], the Naishi were female shrine priests ([[:en:Miko|Miko]]). Their duties included participating in rituals, reciting {{ill|Imayō|ja|今様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imayō|1=imayō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985039}} to entertain noble guests who lodged at the shrine, and performing {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bugaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舞|1=mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109903880}}. According to the "Itsukushima Michishiba-ki" ({{ill|厳島道芝記|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/厳島道芝記|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine maintained a hierarchical structure of Naishi from first Rank (一﨟) to eighth Rank (八﨟), collectively referred to as the "Yaotome" (八乙女) or "Hon-Naishi" (本内侍). Additional titles such as "Wagoto-Naishi" (和琴内侍) and "Karagami-Naishi" (韓神内侍) indicate a system of more than ten positions.
It is also said that {{ill|Mikohimegimi|ja|御子姫君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikohimegimi|1=御子姫君|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488907}}, the seventh daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, was born to Kiyomori and a Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine during his tenure as the governor of Aki Province.

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内侍]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内侍]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内侍]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11393857]] Wikidata name: naishi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Naishi in Japan|Naesi in Korea|{{ill|Eunuch#Korea|ja|宦官#朝鮮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eunuch#Korea|1=eunuch official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24841818}}}} '''Naishi''' (内侍) refers to a category of {{ill|female positions|ja|女性職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/female positions|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in premodern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The term "Naishi" encompasses the following:
# The female officials who served in the {{ill|Naishi-no-tsukasa|ja|内侍司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-no-tsukasa|1=Naishi-no-tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}}, an office of the {{ill|Imperial Court|ja|後宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|1=back palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5353683}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}. # A type of female official in the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}}. # A type of female official in the {{ill|Saiin|ja|斎院司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saiin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. # A type of {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko}} (shrine maiden) at the {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province}}.
== Naishi of the Naishi-no-tsukasa == The Naishi who served in the {{ill|Naishi-no-tsukasa|ja|内侍司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-no-tsukasa|1=Naishi-no-tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}} were tasked with closely attending to the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor}}, handling duties such as mediating {{ill|sōjō|ja|奏上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上奏|1=sōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358776}} (reports to the emperor), conveying the emperor's {{ill|Senka|ja|宣下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senka|1=Senka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452917}} (imperial decrees), and managing the {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro|ja|賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|2=ja|3=賢所|4=fr|5=Kashiko-dokoro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|qq=}}.
The head of the Naishi-no-tsukasa was called the {{ill|Shōji|ja|尚侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōji|1=Naishi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11039254}} (alternatively read as "Naishi-no-kami"), with a limit of two appointments. The second-Ranking officials were the {{ill|Tenji|ja|典侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji|1=Naishi-no-suke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10894614}} (Naishi-no-suke), with four appointments, while the third-Ranking officials, known as {{ill|Shōji|ja|掌侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōji|1=Naishi-no-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498168}} (Naishi-no-jō), could also be referred to as "Naishi" in a narrower sense. There were four Shōji positions. Although there were no {{ill|Shuten|ja|主典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四等官|1=shitōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}} (clerks), the office followed a {{ill|Four-level official hierarchy|ja|四等官|1=shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-level official hierarchy|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan制|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}}.
In addition, there were {{ill|Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja|権掌侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ("provisional Naishi-no-jō") positions with two appointments, as well as an ad hoc position called {{ill|Naishi-dai|ja|内侍代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-dai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to temporarily replace absent Naishi. Although no Naishi roles were directly assigned to the {{ill|Crown Prince's Household|ja|春宮坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crown Prince's Household|1=Crown Prince's Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513463}}, the Naishi-dai was permanently stationed there and would typically transition to a full Naishi position upon the prince’s enthronement.
== Naishi of the Saikū == These Naishi served in close proximity to the {{ill|Saikū|en|Saikū|WD=Q150845|lt=Saikū}}, attending to her needs and responsibilities.
== Naishi of the Saiin == Similarly, the Naishi in the {{ill|Saiin (priestess)|en|Saiin (priestess)|WD=Q7400102|lt=Saiin}} served the {{ill|Saiō|en|Saiō|WD=Q19841349|lt=Saio}} in a comparable capacity to those of the Saikū.
== Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine == At {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine}}, the Naishi were female shrine priests ({{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko}}). Their duties included participating in rituals, reciting {{ill|Imayō|ja|今様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imayō|1=imayō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985039}} to entertain noble guests who lodged at the shrine, and performing {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bugaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舞|1=mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109903880}}. According to the "Itsukushima Michishiba-ki" ({{ill|厳島道芝記|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/厳島道芝記|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine maintained a hierarchical structure of Naishi from first Rank (一﨟) to eighth Rank (八﨟), collectively referred to as the "Yaotome" (八乙女) or "Hon-Naishi" (本内侍). Additional titles such as "Wagoto-Naishi" (和琴内侍) and "Karagami-Naishi" (韓神内侍) indicate a system of more than ten positions.
It is also said that {{ill|Mikohimegimi|ja|御子姫君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikohimegimi|1=御子姫君|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488907}}, the seventh daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, was born to Kiyomori and a Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine during his tenure as the governor of Aki Province.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|author=[[Miyachi Naoichi]] and [[Saeki Ariyoshi]] (supervisors)|title=Shinto Daijiten (Abridged Edition)|publisher=[[Rinsen Shoten]]|date=1969|isbn=4-653-01347-0|original=Heibonsha, 1940}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|内侍|version=86656470|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内侍]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内侍]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内侍]]
{{otheruses|Naishi in Japan|Naesi in Korea|{{ill|Eunuch#Korea|ja|宦官#朝鮮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eunuch#Korea|1=eunuch official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24841818}}}} '''Naishi''' (内侍) refers to a category of {{ill|female positions|ja|女性職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/female positions|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in premodern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The term "Naishi" encompasses the following:
# The female officials who served in the {{ill|Naishi-no-tsukasa|ja|内侍司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-no-tsukasa|1=Naishi-no-tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}}, an office of the {{ill|Imperial Court|ja|後宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|1=back palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5353683}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}. # A type of female official in the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}}. # A type of female official in the {{ill|Saiin|ja|斎院司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saiin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. # A type of {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko}} (shrine maiden) at the {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province}}.
== Naishi of the Naishi-no-tsukasa == The Naishi who served in the {{ill|Naishi-no-tsukasa|ja|内侍司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-no-tsukasa|1=Naishi-no-tsukasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}} were tasked with closely attending to the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor}}, handling duties such as mediating {{ill|sōjō|ja|奏上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上奏|1=sōjō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358776}} (reports to the emperor), conveying the emperor's {{ill|Senka|ja|宣下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senka|1=Senka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452917}} (imperial decrees), and managing the {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro|ja|賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|2=ja|3=賢所|4=fr|5=Kashiko-dokoro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|qq=}}.
The head of the Naishi-no-tsukasa was called the {{ill|Shōji|ja|尚侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōji|1=Naishi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11039254}} (alternatively read as "Naishi-no-kami"), with a limit of two appointments. The second-Ranking officials were the {{ill|Tenji|ja|典侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji|1=Naishi-no-suke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10894614}} (Naishi-no-suke), with four appointments, while the third-Ranking officials, known as {{ill|Shōji|ja|掌侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōji|1=Naishi-no-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498168}} (Naishi-no-jō), could also be referred to as "Naishi" in a narrower sense. There were four Shōji positions. Although there were no {{ill|Shuten|ja|主典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四等官|1=shitōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}} (clerks), the office followed a {{ill|Four-level official hierarchy|ja|四等官|1=shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-level official hierarchy|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan制|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}}.
In addition, there were {{ill|Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja|権掌侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ("provisional Naishi-no-jō") positions with two appointments, as well as an ad hoc position called {{ill|Naishi-dai|ja|内侍代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-dai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to temporarily replace absent Naishi. Although no Naishi roles were directly assigned to the {{ill|Crown Prince's Household|ja|春宮坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crown Prince's Household|1=Crown Prince's Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513463}}, the Naishi-dai was permanently stationed there and would typically transition to a full Naishi position upon the prince’s enthronement.
== Naishi of the Saikū == These Naishi served in close proximity to the {{ill|Saikū|en|Saikū|WD=Q150845|lt=Saikū}}, attending to her needs and responsibilities.
== Naishi of the Saiin == Similarly, the Naishi in the {{ill|Saiin (priestess)|en|Saiin (priestess)|WD=Q7400102|lt=Saiin}} served the {{ill|Saiō|en|Saiō|WD=Q19841349|lt=Saio}} in a comparable capacity to those of the Saikū.
== Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine == At {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine}}, the Naishi were female shrine priests ({{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko}}). Their duties included participating in rituals, reciting {{ill|Imayō|ja|今様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imayō|1=imayō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985039}} to entertain noble guests who lodged at the shrine, and performing {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bugaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舞|1=mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109903880}}. According to the "Itsukushima Michishiba-ki" ({{ill|厳島道芝記|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/厳島道芝記|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine maintained a hierarchical structure of Naishi from first Rank (一﨟) to eighth Rank (八﨟), collectively referred to as the "Yaotome" (八乙女) or "Hon-Naishi" (本内侍). Additional titles such as "Wagoto-Naishi" (和琴内侍) and "Karagami-Naishi" (韓神内侍) indicate a system of more than ten positions.
It is also said that {{ill|Mikohimegimi|ja|御子姫君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikohimegimi|1=御子姫君|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488907}}, the seventh daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, was born to Kiyomori and a Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine during his tenure as the governor of Aki Province.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|author=[[Miyachi Naoichi]] and [[Saeki Ariyoshi]] (supervisors)|title=Shinto Daijiten (Abridged Edition)|publisher=[[Rinsen Shoten]]|date=1969|isbn=4-653-01347-0|original=Heibonsha, 1940}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|内侍|version=86656470|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{otheruses|Naishi in Japan|Naesi in Korea|{{ill|Eunuch#Korea|ja|宦官#朝鮮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eunuch#Korea|1=Eunuch official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24841818}}}} '''Naishi''' (内侍) refers to a category of {{ill|female positions|ja|女性職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/female positions|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in premodern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The term "Naishi" encompasses the following:
# The female officials who served in the {{ill|Naishi-no-tsukasa|ja|内侍司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-no-tsukasa|1=Q9404895|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}}, an office of the {{ill|Imperial Court|ja|後宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|1=back palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5353683}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}. # A type of female official in the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}}. # A type of female official in the {{ill|Saiin|ja|斎院司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saiin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. # A type of {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko|1=Miko}} (shrine maiden) at the {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|WD=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}.
== Naishi of the Naishi-no-tsukasa == The Naishi who served in the {{ill|Naishi-no-tsukasa|ja|内侍司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-no-tsukasa|1=Q9404895|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9404895}} were tasked with closely attending to the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}}, handling duties such as mediating {{ill|sōjō|ja|奏上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上奏|1=Q11358776|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358776}} (reports to the emperor), conveying the emperor's {{ill|Senka|ja|宣下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senka|1=senge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452917}} (imperial decrees), and managing the {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro|ja|賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|2=ja|3=賢所|4=fr|5=Kashiko-dokoro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|qq=}}.
The head of the Naishi-no-tsukasa was called the {{ill|Shōji|ja|尚侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōji|1=Naishi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11039254}} (alternatively read as "Naishi-no-kami"), with a limit of two appointments. The second-Ranking officials were the {{ill|Tenji|ja|典侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji|1=Naishi-no-suke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10894614}} (Naishi-no-suke), with four appointments, while the third-Ranking officials, known as {{ill|Shōji|ja|掌侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōji|1=Naishi-no-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11498168}} (Naishi-no-jō), could also be referred to as "Naishi" in a narrower sense. There were four Shōji positions. Although there were no {{ill|Shuten|ja|主典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四等官|1=Shitōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}} (clerks), the office followed a {{ill|Four-level official hierarchy|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-level official hierarchy|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan制|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2279539}}.
In addition, there were {{ill|Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja|権掌侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ("provisional Naishi-no-jō") positions with two appointments, as well as an ad hoc position called {{ill|Naishi-dai|ja|内侍代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-dai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to temporarily replace absent Naishi. Although no Naishi roles were directly assigned to the {{ill|Crown Prince's Household|ja|春宮坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crown Prince's Household|1=Crown Prince's Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11513463}}, the Naishi-dai was permanently stationed there and would typically transition to a full Naishi position upon the prince’s enthronement.
== Naishi of the Saikū == These Naishi served in close proximity to the {{ill|Saikū|en|Saikū|WD=Q150845|lt=Saikū|1=Saikū}}, attending to her needs and responsibilities.
== Naishi of the Saiin == Similarly, the Naishi in the {{ill|Saiin (priestess)|en|Saiin (priestess)|WD=Q7400102|lt=Saiin|1=Saiin (priestess)}} served the {{ill|Saiō|en|Saiō|WD=Q19841349|lt=Saio|1=Saiō}} in a comparable capacity to those of the Saikū.
== Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine == At {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|WD=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}}, the Naishi were female shrine priests ({{ill|Miko|en|Miko|WD=Q1047139|lt=Miko|1=Miko}}). Their duties included participating in rituals, reciting {{ill|Imayō|ja|今様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imayō|1=imayō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q985039}} to entertain noble guests who lodged at the shrine, and performing {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bugaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舞|1=mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109903880}}. According to the "Itsukushima Michishiba-ki" ({{ill|厳島道芝記|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/厳島道芝記|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine maintained a hierarchical structure of Naishi from first Rank (一﨟) to eighth Rank (八﨟), collectively referred to as the "Yaotome" (八乙女) or "Hon-Naishi" (本内侍). Additional titles such as "Wagoto-Naishi" (和琴内侍) and "Karagami-Naishi" (韓神内侍) indicate a system of more than ten positions.
It is also said that {{ill|Mikohimegimi|ja|御子姫君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikohimegimi|1=Q11488907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488907}}, the seventh daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, was born to Kiyomori and a Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine during his tenure as the governor of Aki Province.

{{otheruses|Naishi in Japan|Naesi in Korea|{{ill|Eunuch#Korea|ja|宦官#朝鮮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eunuch#Korea|1=Eunuch official|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24841818}}}} '''Naishi''' (内侍) refers to a category of {{ill|female positions|ja|女性職|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/female positions|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in premodern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The term "Naishi" encompasses the following:
# The female officials who served in the {{ill|Naishi-no-tsukasa|ja|内侍司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-no-tsukasa|1=Q9404895|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9404895}}, an office of the {{ill|Imperial Court|ja|後宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Court|1=back palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q5353683}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|qid=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}. # A type of female official in the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}}. # A type of female official in the {{ill|Saiin|ja|斎院司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saiin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. # A type of {{ill|Miko|en|Miko|qid=Q1047139|lt=Miko|1=Miko}} (shrine maiden) at the {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|qid=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} in {{ill|Aki Province|en|Aki Province|qid=Q965029|lt=Aki Province|1=Aki Province}}.
== Naishi of the Naishi-no-tsukasa == The Naishi who served in the {{ill|Naishi-no-tsukasa|ja|内侍司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-no-tsukasa|1=Q9404895|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9404895}} were tasked with closely attending to the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}}, handling duties such as mediating {{ill|sōjō|ja|奏上|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sōjō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 上奏|1=Q11358776|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11358776}} (reports to the emperor), conveying the emperor's {{ill|Senka|ja|宣下|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senka|1=senge|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452917}} (imperial decrees), and managing the {{ill|Kashiko-dokoro|ja|賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|2=ja|3=賢所|4=fr|5=Kashiko-dokoro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashiko-dokoro|qq=}}.
The head of the Naishi-no-tsukasa was called the {{ill|Shōji|ja|尚侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōji|1=Naishi-no-kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11039254}} (alternatively read as "Naishi-no-kami"), with a limit of two appointments. The second-Ranking officials were the {{ill|Tenji|ja|典侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenji|1=Naishi-no-suke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10894614}} (Naishi-no-suke), with four appointments, while the third-Ranking officials, known as {{ill|Shōji|ja|掌侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōji|1=Naishi-no-jō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11498168}} (Naishi-no-jō), could also be referred to as "Naishi" in a narrower sense. There were four Shōji positions. Although there were no {{ill|Shuten|ja|主典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 四等官|1=Shitōkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2279539}} (clerks), the office followed a {{ill|Four-level official hierarchy|ja|四等官|1=Shitōkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four-level official hierarchy|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitōkan制|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2279539}}.
In addition, there were {{ill|Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja|権掌侍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gon-no-naishi-no-jō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ("provisional Naishi-no-jō") positions with two appointments, as well as an ad hoc position called {{ill|Naishi-dai|ja|内侍代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi-dai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to temporarily replace absent Naishi. Although no Naishi roles were directly assigned to the {{ill|Crown Prince's Household|ja|春宮坊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crown Prince's Household|1=Crown Prince's Household|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11513463}}, the Naishi-dai was permanently stationed there and would typically transition to a full Naishi position upon the prince’s enthronement.
== Naishi of the Saikū == These Naishi served in close proximity to the {{ill|Saikū|en|Saikū|qid=Q150845|lt=Saikū|1=Saikū}}, attending to her needs and responsibilities.
== Naishi of the Saiin == Similarly, the Naishi in the {{ill|Saiin (priestess)|en|Saiin (priestess)|qid=Q7400102|lt=Saiin|1=Saiin (priestess)}} served the {{ill|Saiō|en|Saiō|qid=Q19841349|lt=Saio|1=Saiō}} in a comparable capacity to those of the Saikū.
== Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine == At {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|en|Itsukushima Shrine|qid=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}}, the Naishi were female shrine priests ({{ill|Miko|en|Miko|qid=Q1047139|lt=Miko|1=Miko}}). Their duties included participating in rituals, reciting {{ill|Imayō|ja|今様|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imayō|1=imayō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q985039}} to entertain noble guests who lodged at the shrine, and performing {{ill|bugaku|ja|舞楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bugaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 舞|1=mai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109903880}}. According to the "Itsukushima Michishiba-ki" ({{ill|厳島道芝記|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/厳島道芝記|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), the shrine maintained a hierarchical structure of Naishi from first Rank (一﨟) to eighth Rank (八﨟), collectively referred to as the "Yaotome" (八乙女) or "Hon-Naishi" (本内侍). Additional titles such as "Wagoto-Naishi" (和琴内侍) and "Karagami-Naishi" (韓神内侍) indicate a system of more than ten positions.
It is also said that {{ill|Mikohimegimi|ja|御子姫君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikohimegimi|1=Q11488907|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11488907}}, the seventh daughter of [[Taira no Kiyomori]]{{citation needed |Taira no Kiyomori|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Kiyomori|qq=}}, was born to Kiyomori and a Naishi of Itsukushima Shrine during his tenure as the governor of Aki Province.
